NCT07262619.title: EIK1005-002: A Clinical Research Study Evaluating EIK1005, a Werner Helicase Inhibitor, as Monotherapy and in Combination With Pembrolizumab in Participants With Advanced Solid Tumors Including Microsatellite Instability High (MSI-H) Tumors

eligibility.text: . is ≥ 18 years of age at the time of signing the informed consent.
eligibility.justification: Age requirement ensures participants are legally competent to participate.

eligibility.text: . has a life expectancy of at least 3 months.
eligibility.justification: Ensures patients have sufficient time for treatment and potential outcomes.

eligibility.text: has histologically or cytologically documented advanced (unresectable and/or metastatic) solid tumor. Part 1A: recommend that participants have archival tissue not more than 3 years old.
eligibility.justification: Ensures the study population is consistent with the diagnosis of advanced cancer.

eligibility.text: Part 1A: recommend that participants have archival tissue not more than 3 years old.
eligibility.justification: Archival tissue requirement ensures sufficient time for tumor analysis and diagnosis.

eligibility.text: Part 1B and Part 2: participant has locally confirmed Microsatellite Instability High (MSI-H) or Mismatch Repair Deficient (dMMR) tumor.
eligibility.justification: Identifies patients with specific tumor characteristics for targeted treatment.

eligibility.text: Participant must have archival tumor tissue (not more than 3 years old) for retrospective confirmation of MSI-H or dMMR tumor by a central laboratory.
eligibility.justification: Ensures accurate diagnosis and treatment selection.

eligibility.text: . In Part 1A, has received and then progressed after or is intolerant to at least 1 standard treatment regimen in the advanced setting. The participant does not have alternative therapeutic options per PI's medical judgement.
eligibility.justification: Ensures patients are unable to tolerate other treatments.

eligibility.text: The participant does not have alternative therapeutic options per PI's medical judgement.
eligibility.justification: Provides a clear rationale for the study design and eligibility criteria.

eligibility.text: . has measurable disease at baseline according to Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumors version 1.1 (RECIST 1.1) as determined by the PI.
eligibility.justification: Ensures a clear definition of disease progression and treatment response.

eligibility.text: . has an Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) score of 0 to 1.
eligibility.justification: Assesses the patient's overall health status for study participation.

eligibility.text: . has an adequate organ and marrow function.
eligibility.justification: Ensures patients have sufficient organ function to tolerate treatment.

eligibility.text: . has not recovered (i.e., to Grade ≤ 1 or to baseline) from prior anti-cancer therapy-induced adverse events (AEs).
eligibility.justification: Ensures patients have recovered sufficiently from previous treatments.

eligibility.text: has received prior treatment with Werner (WRN) inhibitor.
eligibility.justification: Identifies patients who have already been exposed to WRN inhibitors, potentially impacting study outcomes.

eligibility.text: . has a history of relevant drug hypersensitivity, ascertained or presumptive allergy/hypersensitivity to the active drug substance and/or formulation ingredients, history of serious allergic reactions leading to hospitalization, or any other allergic reaction in general.
eligibility.justification: Identifies patients with potential allergies that could impact study outcomes.

eligibility.text: . In Parts 1B and Part 2 Rescue: diagnosis of immunodeficiency or is receiving chronic systemic steroid therapy (in dosing exceeding 10 mg daily of prednisone equivalent) or any other form of immunosuppressive therapy within 7 days prior to the first dose of study intervention.
eligibility.justification: Identifies patients with potential immune system deficiencies that could impact study outcomes.

eligibility.text: . has known additional malignancy that is progressing or has required active treatment within the past 3 years.
eligibility.justification: Ensures patients are not at risk of developing new malignancies during the study.

eligibility.text: . has known active central nervous system (CNS) metastases and/or carcinomatous meningitis.
eligibility.justification: Identifies patients with potential CNS complications that could impact study outcomes.

eligibility.text: Participants with previously treated brain metastases may participate provided they are radiologically stable (ie, without evidence of progression) for at least 4 weeks as confirmed by repeat imaging performed during the study screening, are clinically stable and have not required steroid treatment for at least 14 days before the first dose of study treatment.
eligibility.justification: Ensures patients with previous brain metastases are monitored for potential complications.

eligibility.text: . has mean resting QTcF > 470 ms (men and women) obtained from triplicate electrocardiograms (ECGs).
eligibility.justification: Ensures patients have a normal heart rhythm to avoid potential complications.

eligibility.text: . has active autoimmune disease that has required systemic treatment in the past 2 years (ie, with use of disease modifying agents, corticosteroids, or immunosuppressive drugs). Participants may enroll with the following conditions: Type 1 diabetes, hypothyroidism requiring hormone replacement, or skin disorders (vitiligo, psoriasis, or alopecia not requiring systemic treatment).
eligibility.justification: Identifies patients with potential autoimmune complications that could impact study outcomes.

eligibility.text: . has history of (non-infectious) pneumonitis/pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease that required steroids or current pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease.
eligibility.justification: Identifies patients with potential respiratory complications that could impact study outcomes.

eligibility.text: . has active tuberculosis.
eligibility.justification: Ensures patients are not at risk of developing active TB during the study.

eligibility.text: . has any active infections requiring systemic therapy.
eligibility.justification: Identifies patients with potential infection complications that could impact study outcomes.

power_analysis.assessment: N/A — Phase 1 trial, not powered for efficacy

endpoints.timeframe: 21 Days

endpoints.text: Adverse Events (AEs) - Part 1 and Part 2
endpoints.timeframe: From the time of first dose of study medication through 30 days following cessation of study treatment.

endpoints.text: Objective Response (OR) - Part 1 and Part 2
endpoints.timeframe: Through study completion, an average of 2 years.

endpoints.text: Duration of Response (DOR) - Part 1 and Part 2
endpoints.timeframe: Through study completion, an average of 2 years.

endpoints.text: Disease Control (DC) - Part 1 and Part 2
endpoints.timeframe: Through study completion, an average of 2 years.

endpoints.text: Progression-free survival (PFS) - Part 2
endpoints.timeframe: Through study completion, an average of 2 years.

endpoints.text: Pharmacokinetic (PK) parameters of EIK1005 - AUC0-24 (Part 1 and Part 2)
endpoints.timeframe: Up to 1 year

endpoints.text: Pharmacokinetic (PK) parameters of EIK1005 - AUCtau,ss (Part 1 and Part 2)
endpoints.timeframe: Up to 1 year

endpoints.text: Pharmacokinetic (PK) parameters of EIK1005 - Cmax (Part 1 and Part 2)
endpoints.timeframe: Up to 1 year

endpoints.text: Pharmacokinetic (PK) parameters of EIK1005 - tmax (Part 1 and Part 2)
endpoints.timeframe: Up to 1 year

endpoints.text: Pharmacokinetic (PK) parameters of EIK1005 - t½ (Half-life) (Part 1 and Part 2)
endpoints.timeframe: Up to 1 year

randomization.randomization_description: Stratified by Baseline, Prior Treatment, Age.

adaptive_designs.description: Adaptive design detected: Types identified: Dose-Finding / Phase I.

safety_adverse_events.ae_ascertainment: Not explicitly described (assumed standard investigator reporting)
safety_adverse_events.safety_monitoring: Investigator-reported with sponsor oversight (no DSMB mentioned)

pharmacogenetics.genetic_screen_failure_summary: Detected 1 genetic biomarker requirement(s) in eligibility: dMMR_MSI_H (pop. prevalence ~15%, ~85% screen fail). Worst-case screen failure rate from genetic criteria: ~85%.
pharmacogenetics.summary: Genes detected (PD1, PDL1, CTLA4) but could not map indication "advanced solid tumors" to a GWAS EFO trait. Try alternative trait name.

relaxations.description: Decrease minimum age from 18 to 13 years
relaxations.raw_text: 1. is ≥ 18 years of age at the time of signing the informed consent.

NCT06710847.title: A Study to Investigate the Safety, Pharmacokinetics, and Preliminary Effectiveness of GSK4418959 Alone or in Combination With Other Anti-cancer Agents in Participants With Solid Tumors

eligibility.text: Has a histologically diagnosed advanced (unresectable, metastatic or recurrent) solid tumor
eligibility.justification: This criterion protects patients from harm by ensuring they have a confirmed diagnosis of an advanced solid tumor.

eligibility.text: Has a known dMMR/MSI-H status as determined by a certified local laboratory at the time of Pre-screening or has an unknown Mismatch repair (MMR)/ Microsatellite Instability (MSI) status at the time of Pre-screening and MMR/MSI status will be determined by central reference laboratory
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a homogenous population for comparison, maximizing the likelihood of detecting treatment effects.

eligibility.text: Provides an archival or fresh (preferred) formalin fixed, paraffin embedded (FFPE) sample
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures adequate tissue samples for analysis and diagnosis.

eligibility.text: Intends to receive GSK4418959 (alone or in combination with PD-1 inhibitor, as determined between Investigator and sponsor) as next line of treatment
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient's treatment plan aligns with the study protocol.

eligibility.text: Has an Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status of 0-2
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients are physically capable of participating in the trial and managing potential side effects.

eligibility.text: Is expected to have a minimum of 3 months life expectancy
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patient survival is sufficient for the study duration.

eligibility.text: Has adequate organ function, as defined in the protocol
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have sufficient organ function to tolerate treatment and participate in the trial.

eligibility.text: • Has histologically diagnosed advanced (unresectable, metastatic or recurrent) solid tumor and has exhausted all standard of care treatment options
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have exhausted other treatment options before participating in the trial.

eligibility.text: Has histologically diagnosed advanced (unresectable, metastatic or recurrent) Colorectal cancer (CRC) or Endometrial cancer (EC)
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a homogenous population for comparison and maximizes the likelihood of detecting treatment effects.

eligibility.text: Has received at least 1 but no more than 3 lines of systemic anticancer therapy for their advanced (unresectable, metastatic or recurrent) disease including at least one line of Immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICI) therapy
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have received a sufficient number of treatment lines to allow for comparison with the study drug.

eligibility.text: Has measurable disease (i.e., at least 1 target lesion) during the Screening period per RECIST 1.1, as determined by the investigator
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have a measurable disease to allow for comparison with the study drug.

eligibility.text: Has not recovered (i.e., to Grade ≤1 or to baseline) from prior anticancer therapy-induced AEs
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have recovered sufficiently from previous treatment side effects before participating in the trial.

eligibility.text: Has received prior treatment with a WRN inhibitor
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have not received WRN inhibitors, which could confound the study results.

eligibility.text: Is unable to swallow and retain orally administered study treatment
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patient compliance with the study protocol.

eligibility.text: Has symptomatic uncontrolled brain or leptomeningeal metastases
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have a condition that could be detrimental to their health and safety.

eligibility.text: Has a known additional malignancy that progressed or required active treatment within the last 2 years because reoccurrence of another malignancy would confound interpretation by RECIST 1.1 criteria. Exceptions include basal cell carcinoma of the skin, squamous cell carcinoma of the skin that has undergone potentially curative therapy, or in situ cancer that is considered to be low risk for progression by the investigator
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have a condition that could be detrimental to their health and safety.

eligibility.text: Has any impairment of gastrointestinal function or gastrointestinal disease that may significantly alter the absorption of study drugs
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have a condition that could affect their ability to participate in the trial.

eligibility.text: Has severe liver fibrosis
eligibility.justification: This criterion protects patients from harm by ensuring they have a condition that could be detrimental to their health and safety.

eligibility.text: Has cirrhosis or current unstable liver or biliary disease
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have a condition that could be detrimental to their health and safety.

eligibility.text: Has known hypersensitivity to any of the study interventions or any of their excipients
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have a condition that could be detrimental to their health and safety.

eligibility.text: Has known WRN syndrome
eligibility.justification: This criterion protects patients from harm by excluding those with a known genetic disorder.

eligibility.text: Has an active autoimmune disease that has required systemic treatment in the past 2 years
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures feasibility by limiting the study population to those with a history of active autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment.

eligibility.text: Has experienced any of the following with prior immunotherapy: any immune mediated adverse events (imAE) of Grade ≥3, immune-related severe neurologic events of any grade, exfoliative dermatitis of any grade (Stevens-Johnson Syndrome, toxic epidermal necrolysis, or Drug rash with eosinophilia and systemic signs syndrome /[DRESS/] syndrome), or myocarditis of any grade.
eligibility.justification: This criterion prioritizes patient safety by excluding individuals who have experienced severe immune-related adverse events after prior immunotherapy.

eligibility.text: Has any history of interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis
eligibility.justification: This criterion protects patients from harm by excluding those with a history of interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis, which can be life-threatening.

power_analysis.assessment: N/A — Phase 1 trial, not powered for efficacy

NCT06898450.title: A Study to Assess the Safety, Tolerability, and Efficacy of NDI-219216 in Patients With Advanced Solid Tumors.

eligibility.text: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status score of 0 or 1
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients can participate in the trial and are able to tolerate the study's demands.

eligibility.text: Have unresectable and/or metastatic solid tumors (with or without MSI-H/dMMR) refractory to or intolerant to previous SoC therapy or for which no SoC therapy exists
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is representative of a specific patient group and allows for comparison with historical data.

eligibility.text: Presence of measurable disease according to RECIST version 1.1 except for Part A (Dose Escalation)
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is representative of a specific patient group and allows for comparison with historical data.

eligibility.text: Adequate bone marrow / hematologic, end-organ, and cardiovascular function
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is healthy enough to tolerate the study's demands.

eligibility.text: Resolution of all acute (or toxic) adverse effects of prior therapies, radiation therapy, or surgical procedures to Grade ≤ 1 (except fatigue, alopecia, and peripheral neuropathy)
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is healthy enough to tolerate the study's demands.

eligibility.text: Clinically significant cardiovascular disease.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is healthy enough to tolerate the study's demands.

eligibility.text: Patients with known WRN syndrome
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is healthy enough to tolerate the study's demands.

eligibility.text: Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or intention of becoming pregnant during the study.
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes women who are pregnant or may become pregnant during the study. This is a feasibility concern for recruitment and data quality.

power_analysis.assessment: N/A — Phase 1 trial, not powered for efficacy

endpoints.text: Part A Primary Objective: Incidence of dose limiting toxicities (DLTs)
endpoints.timeframe: The first 21 days of Cycle 1 (Cycle 1 is 28 days).

endpoints.text: Part A Primary Outcome: • Incidence and severity of Adverse Events (AEs) and Serious Adverse Events (SAEs), according to NCI CTCAE v5.0
endpoints.timeframe: From first dose of study drug until 30 days after last dose of study drug; up to approximately 11-12 months. Each Cycle is 28 days.

endpoints.text: Part A Primary Outcome: Incidence and severity of Treatment Emergent Adverse Events (TEAEs) and Treatment Related Adverse Events (TRAEs) as assessed by the Investigator
endpoints.timeframe: From first dose of study drug until 30 days after last dose of study drug; up to approximately 11-12 months. Each Cycle is 28 days.

endpoints.text: Part B Primary Objective: Overall Response Rate (ORR) per RECIST v1.1.
endpoints.timeframe: From start of study treatment until end of follow-up, up to approximately 18 months. Each Cycle is 28 days.

endpoints.text: Part B Primary Outcome: Duration of Response (DOR) per RECIST v1.1
endpoints.timeframe: From the time of first occurrence of a documented response until the time of documented disease progression or death from any cause, whichever occurs first; up to approximately 18 months. Each Cycle is 28 days.

endpoints.text: Part B Primary Outcome: Incidence and severity of AEs according to NCI CTCAE v5.0.
endpoints.timeframe: From first dose of study drug until 30 days after last dose of study drug; up to approximately 18 months. Each Cycle is 28 days.

endpoints.text: Part C Primary Objective: Overall Response Rate (ORR) per RECIST v1.1.
endpoints.timeframe: From start of study treatment until end of follow-up, up to approximately 17 months. Each Cycle is 28 days.

endpoints.text: Part C Primary Outcome: Duration of Response (DOR) per RECIST v1.1.
endpoints.timeframe: From the time of first occurrence of a documented response until the time of documented disease progression or death from any cause, whichever occurs first, up to approximately 17 months. Each Cycle is 28 days.

endpoints.text: Part A Secondary Objective: Plasma concentrations of NDI-219216 will be measured using a validated liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (LC-MS) assay.
endpoints.timeframe: At pre-specified timepoints on Day 1, Day 2, Day 8, Day 21, and Day 22 of Cycle 1. Cycle 1 is 28 days in length.

endpoints.text: Part A Secondary Outcome: Maximum Plasma Concentration Observed (Cmax) of NDI-219216
endpoints.timeframe: At pre-specified timepoints on Day 1, Day 2, Day 8, Day 21, and Day 22 of Cycle 1. Cycle 1 is 28 days in length.

endpoints.text: Part A Secondary Outcome: Time of Maximum Plasma Concentration Observed (Tmax) of NDI-219216
endpoints.timeframe: At pre-specified timepoints on Day 1, Day 2, Day 8, Day 21, and Day 22 of Cycle 1. Cycle 1 is 28 days in length.

endpoints.text: Part A Secondary Outcome: Area Under the Plasma Concentration-Time Curve (AUC0-last) of NDI-219216
endpoints.timeframe: At pre-specified timepoints on Day 1, Day 2, Day 8, Day 21, and Day 22 of Cycle 1 from time zero to the last observable concentration. Cycle 1 is 28 days in length.

endpoints.text: Part B Secondary Objective: Plasma concentrations of NDI-219216 will be measured using a validated liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (LC-MS) assay.
endpoints.timeframe: At pre-specified timepoints on Day 1, Day 2, Day 8, Day 21, and Day 22 of Cycle 1. Cycle 1 is 28 days in length.

endpoints.timeframe: At pre-specified timepoints on Day 1, Day 2, Day 8, Day 21, and Day 22 of Cycle 1. Cycle 1 is 28 days in length.

endpoints.timeframe: From first dose of study drug until 30 days after last dose of study drug; up to approximately 17 months. Each Cycle is 28 days.
